title: Email notifications arriving hours late
body: Task assignment notifications used to arrive within a minute or two. For the past week, they've been arriving 2-4 hours late. This is causing people to miss deadlines because they don't know they've been assigned a task until hours later. It seems to be worse in the morning — notifications sent around 9-10am take the longest to arrive. Afternoon notifications are usually faster but still delayed.

ground_truth.description: The reporter is a team lead who relies on email notifications for task assignments. TaskFlow uses a priority queue for email delivery. One week ago, a product change added a daily marketing digest email feature that sends a batch of ~10,000 digest emails at 9am. The digest emails were accidentally given the same priority level (priority=1) as transactional notifications (task assignments, due date reminders). The queue processes emails in priority order, and within the same priority, in FIFO order. So the 10K digest emails queued at 9am push all subsequent transactional emails behind a multi-hour backlog. By afternoon, the digest batch is mostly drained and transactional emails flow faster. The queue worker processes ~100 emails per minute. Before the digest feature, the queue had at most 50-100 emails at any time and transactional notifications went out in under a minute.
ground_truth.actual_cause: Priority inversion in the email queue. Marketing digest emails (bulk, low-urgency) share the same priority level as transactional notifications (high-urgency). The 10K digest batch queued at 9am creates a multi-hour backlog for transactional emails. The fix is to give digest emails a lower priority (e.g., priority=5) so transactional emails (priority=1) are always processed first.
acceptable_paths: Identifying the 9am timing pattern and suspecting a batch job or scheduled task that floods the email pipeline, even without identifying the priority queue mechanism
Identifying the digest email feature as the cause and the queue congestion, even without understanding the priority system
ground_truth.environment: TaskFlow v2.3.1, email via SMTP relay, priority queue for email delivery, ~200 users, ~10K daily digest emails
ground_truth.workaround: Temporarily disable the marketing digest feature; or manually check the TaskFlow dashboard for new assignments

expected_triage_extracts: worse in the morning (9-10am), better in the afternoon
related to a batch email process (marketing digest)
transactional and bulk emails competing for the same pipeline
notifications used to arrive within 1-2 minutes
